title: maughania oblongifolia
intro: Maughania oblongifolia, also known as Kuikui or Kampila, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. Maughania oblongifolia is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including powder, decoction, and extract, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, diabetes, and liver and kidney disorders.
